request.question: Which network does the series created by a man born with the name Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible appear on?

title: Los Grutynos
Ben M. Baglio
Johnny Bravo
Van Partible
77 Sunset Strip
New Amsterdam (TV series)
List of Johnny Bravo episodes
List of GetBackers episodes
Odd Mom Out
Ali Saleem
sentences: Los Grutynos is an Argentine comic strip about a fictional family which come from the bottom of the sea of the City of Grutópolys, located in the "Bahía sin Fondo" (Bottomless Bay), San Matías Gulf, in Patagonia Argentina, created by Beto Noy, a business man born in the Río Negro province.
Ben M. Baglio (born 1960 in New York), created the brief for two series of children's books – "Dolphin Diaries" and "Animal Ark".
"Dolphin Diaries" features a girl and her family from Florida, who travel around the world as marine biologists and study dolphins.
"Animal Ark" features two children who work together to help animals and solve animal-related mysteries.
The books were written by commissioned writers in the UK under Baglio's instruction and published using the pseudonym Lucy Daniels in the UK.
Each author is named with a 'Special Thanks' on the copyright page – see Dolphin in the Deep copyright page for an example.
In the USA the books are published under the name of Ben M. Baglio so this name is effectively both a man born in 1960 and also the collective pseudonym for the group of writers who write the books.
Johnny Bravo is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for Cartoon Network, and the second of the network's Cartoon Cartoons.
The series centers on the title character, a muscular and boorish young man who tries to get women to date him, though he is usually unsuccessful.
He ends up in bizarre situations and predicaments, often accompanied by celebrity guest characters such as Donny Osmond or Adam West.
Throughout its run, the show was controversial for its adult humor, pop culture references, and sly adult-oriented jokes.
Van Partible (born Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible on December 13, 1971) is a Filipino-born American animator, writer and producer best known for creating the animated television series "Johnny Bravo".
77 Sunset Strip is an American television private detective series created by Roy Huggins and starring Efrem Zimbalist Jr., Roger Smith, and Edd Byrnes.
Each episode was one hour long.
New Amsterdam is an American television drama which aired for eight episodes in 2008 on Fox.

The series starred Nikolaj Coster-Waldau as "John Amsterdam" (real name Johann van der Zee), an immortal Dutch man born in 1607, who has lived in New York City on and off since he was 14 years old, and who is a homicide detective in the present day.
The series was nominated for an Emmy for Main Title Design.
"Johnny Bravo" is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for the Cartoon Network.
The series premiered July 14, 1997, and ended officially August 27, 2004.
The series is a spin-off of "World Premiere Toons", also known as the "What a Cartoon!
Show".
The episodes for the anime series "GetBackers" were produced by Studio Deen and based on the manga series of the same name written by Yuya Aoki and illustrated by Rando Ayamine.
The series premiered on Tokyo Broadcasting System in Japan on October 5, 2002 and ran for forty-nine episodes until September 20, 2003 under direction of Kazuhiro Furuhashi and Keitaro Motonaga.
The plot follows the "GetBackers", a group that retrieves anything that was lost.

The team is primary composed by Ban Mido, a man born with the illusionary technique "Evil Eye", and Ginji Amano the former leader of a gang called "The VOLTS", a powerful group in the dangerous territory called the Limitless Fortress in Shinjuku.
Odd Mom Out is an American comedy television series created by and starring Jill Kargman.
A 10-episode first season was ordered by the American cable television network Bravo.

The series focuses on Jill Kargman playing a fictionalized version of herself, Jill Weber, who is forced to navigate the wealthy mommy clique that resides in New York’s prestigious Upper East Side neighborhood.
The principal photography commenced in September 2014; the show is filmed at various locations in New York City.
The series premiered on June 8, 2015.
On September 22, 2016, the network renewed "Odd Mom Out" for a 10-episode third season shortly after the second season concluded.
Ali Saleem (Urdu: علی سلیم), best known by his alter-ego Begum Nawazish Ali, is a Pakistani television host, actor, scriptwriter and impressionist.
He became a contestant in Bigg Boss 4 in 2010.

He broke into the mainstream audiences through his impersonations of late prime minister Benazir Bhutto, and later playing the cross-dressing Begum Nawazish Ali on various television channels, including the Aaj TV network, Dawn News and Geo TV network.
His alter ego of Begum Nawazish Ali has now become his primary persona as he rarely appears as the male Ali Saleem.
He is a bisexual man born to a retired colonel father in Pakistan Army

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- When the context contains a concise bridge claim about a named entity and at least one passage provides an explicit, identity-aligned statement linking that same named entity (or a clearly indicated alias) to the asserted relation, append exactly one brief corroborating sentence that paraphrases that passage's linking fact and references the corroborating passage. Perform an explicit identity-alignment check (alias or explicit linking); do not treat mere name co-occurrence or a passage that merely mentions the name as sufficient. Do not append this corroboration if the `context` already contains the identical corroborating fact verbatim. Keep this addition short and keep the overall summary compact.
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.
- When the summaries contain both a short single-token alias and, in the later integrated summary (summary_2), a contiguous multi-token full-name literal that explicitly identifies or is explicitly linked to the same person (for example via an identity link or biographical framing in summary_2), prefer and emit that multi-token full-name literal exactly as it appears in summary_2. Do not prefer the longer form unless summary_2 provides an explicit identity link tying the alias to the full-name literal; if no such explicit link is present, follow the base identity-link rules and do not force selection of the longer form.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Which network does the series created by a man born with the name Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible appear on?
hop1_docs: Van Partible | Van Partible (born Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible on December 13, 1971) is a Filipino-born American animator, writer and producer best known for creating the animated television series "Johnny Bravo".

Johnny Bravo | Johnny Bravo is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for Cartoon Network, and the second of the network's Cartoon Cartoons.  The series centers on the title character, a muscular and boorish young man who tries to get women to date him, though he is usually unsuccessful.  He ends up in bizarre situations and predicaments, often accompanied by celebrity guest characters such as Donny Osmond or Adam West.  Throughout its run, the show was controversial for its adult humor, pop culture references, and sly adult-oriented jokes.

List of Johnny Bravo episodes | "Johnny Bravo" is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for the Cartoon Network.  The series premiered July 14, 1997, and ended officially August 27, 2004.  The series is a spin-off of "World Premiere Toons", also known as the "What a Cartoon!  Show".

Eddie Bravo Invitational | The Eddie Bravo Invitational (EBI) is an invitational 16-man tournament, featuring submission-only (non-striking) grappling.  Combat Jiu Jitsu, which does allow palm strikes, was added for EBI 11.  The event and its rule-set were founded by Eddie Bravo.

Efrem Zimbalist Jr. | Efrem Zimbalist Jr. (November 30, 1918 – May 2, 2014) was an American actor known for his starring roles in the television series "77 Sunset Strip" and "The F.B.I." He is also known as recurring character "Dandy Jim Buckley" in the series "Maverick" and as the voice behind the character Alfred Pennyworth in "" and associated spin-offs.  He also voiced Doctor Octopus in the 1990s "Spider-Man" animated series, and Justin Hammer from the second season of the 1994 "Iron Man" animated series.

Perou | Ralph John Perou (born May 1970), known professionally as Perou, is a British fashion, portrait and music photographer who has also appeared as a judge on "Make Me a Supermodel UK" and on season 2 of Bravo TV's American "Make Me a Supermodel".  He was part of the T.V series called "Dirty Sexy Things" on the British network E4 which aired in 2011.  Perou photographed models to be put in his exhibition at the end of the series.

Angie Dickinson | Angie Dickinson (born Angeline Brown, September 30, 1931) is an American actress.  She began her career on television, appearing in many anthology series during the 1950s, before landing her breakthrough role in 1956 in "Gun the Man Down" with James Arness and the 1959 Western film "Rio Bravo", for which she received the Golden Globe Award for New Star of the Year.
response.summary_1: Van Partible (born "Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible on December 13, 1971") is credited with creating the animated series "Johnny Bravo" (passage: "Van Partible ... is ... best known for creating the animated television series 'Johnny Bravo'"). The passages state "'Johnny Bravo' is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for Cartoon Network" (quoted phrase: "created by Van Partible for Cartoon Network"). Bridge entity: the series "Johnny Bravo"; supported missing fact: the network is Cartoon Network. Use the quoted anchors above to retrieve/confirm the network.
response.hop2_query: Johnny Bravo Van Partible Cartoon Network
hop2_docs: List of Johnny Bravo episodes | "Johnny Bravo" is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for the Cartoon Network.  The series premiered July 14, 1997, and ended officially August 27, 2004.  The series is a spin-off of "World Premiere Toons", also known as the "What a Cartoon!  Show".

Johnny Bravo | Johnny Bravo is an American animated television series created by Van Partible for Cartoon Network, and the second of the network's Cartoon Cartoons.  The series centers on the title character, a muscular and boorish young man who tries to get women to date him, though he is usually unsuccessful.  He ends up in bizarre situations and predicaments, often accompanied by celebrity guest characters such as Donny Osmond or Adam West.  Throughout its run, the show was controversial for its adult humor, pop culture references, and sly adult-oriented jokes.

Van Partible | Van Partible (born Efrem Giovanni Bravo Partible on December 13, 1971) is a Filipino-born American animator, writer and producer best known for creating the animated television series "Johnny Bravo".

Cartoon Network: Block Party | Cartoon Network: Block Party is an arcade-style video game for Game Boy Advance developed by Majesco Entertainment and released in the United States on August 5, 2004 and in Europe and Oceania on September 15, 2004.  It features characters from Cartoon Network original animated series such as "Johnny Bravo", "Ed Edd n Eddy", "Courage the Cowardly Dog", and "Cow and Chicken".

Cartoon Network Racing | Cartoon Network Racing is a racing video game developed by Eutechnyx (PS2) and Firebrand Games (Nintendo DS version) and published by The Game Factory on December 4, 2006, in North America, and on February 9, 2007, in Europe.  The gameplay is similar to Nintendo's 2003 game "", but the characters and racetracks are from Cartoon Network's original animated television series.  The six series featured are "Courage the Cowardly Dog", "Cow and Chicken", "Dexter's Laboratory", "I Am Weasel", "Johnny Bravo", and "The Powerpuff Girls".

Cartoon Network Amazone Waterpark | Cartoon Network Amazone Waterpark (Thai: สวนน้ำการ์ตูนเน็ทเวิร์ค อเมโซน) is the world's first Cartoon Network themed waterpark located at 888 Moo 8, Na Jomtien, Sattahip, Chonburi, Thailand, 15 kilometers away from South Pattaya.  The park has recreated an atmosphere of Amazon rainforest with several attractions and activities such as water slides, artificial sea, rafting, flowriding and live shows, all featuring Cartoon Network's famous characters including Ben 10 and his aliens, Adventure Time's Finn and Jake, The Powerpuff Girls, Johnny Bravo etc.

Johnny Test (season 5) | The fifth season of the American animated television series "Johnny Test" originally aired on Cartoon Network in the United States.  The season was announced by Cookie Jar Entertainment on August 24, 2010, consisting of 26 episodes, with two segments each.  In the United States, the season premiered on Cartoon Network on June 13, 2011.
response.summary_2: Johnny Bravo appears on Cartoon Network. Passages [1] and [2] explicitly state that Johnny Bravo was created by Van Partible for Cartoon Network.
